title: docs(ci): record CI-RED-021 reviewer-topology ordering constraint
intent: Record the ordering constraint for full CI-RED-021 closure. Because the active ruleset has no bypass actors and repo admins do not bypass rulesets by default, a second collaborator/org-team CODEOWNER must be added BEFORE requiring approvals + code-owner review; doing it in the wrong order locks the solo owner out of main and forces a self-bypass path that defeats the item.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) SPRINTS.md: append an 'Ordering for full closure' note to the CI-RED-021 ledger row spelling out that the second reviewer must be added before required_approving_review_count>=1 + require_code_owner_review:true are enabled, since bypass_actors is empty and admins do not bypass rulesets. (2) this per-PR CBSP21 manifest.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: The prior CI-RED-004/021 witness recorded WHAT the state is but not the sequencing hazard for closing 021. Without this note a future session could flip the review fields first and lock main, then 'fix' it by adding a bypass actor — reintroducing the routine-bypass path the item exists to close.

files.intent: Append the reviewer-topology ordering constraint to the CI-RED-021 row.
Per-PR CBSP21 manifest declaring the ledger-note patch.
